content.thinking: Let me start by reading the full issue context and understanding what needs to be done.

content.content:
{"body":"From 'Structural and functional insights into the mechanism of action of plant borate transporters' PMID:34112901

There are two types of 'borate' transporters:

channels:
Nodulin 26-like intrinsic proteins (NIPs) (https://tcdb.org/search/result.php?tc=1.A.8.12#1.A.8.12)
carriers which are responsible for the efflux of borate:
BOR/Solute Carrier (SLC) 4 family of secondary active transporters (https://tcdb.org/search/result.php?tc=2.A.31)

The term GO:0046715 'active borate transmembrane transporter activity' is confusing and has been used to annotate NIPs and BOR/Solute Carriers and so should be obsoleted. 


* **GO term ID and Label**
* GO:0046715	active borate transmembrane transporter activity

* **Reason for deprecation** Put an x in the appropriate box: 
 - [x] The reason for obsoletion is that the term is not clearly defined and usage has been inconsistent.
 - [ ]  The reason for obsoletion is that this term is pre-composed and should be represented as a GO-CAM model.
 - [ ]  The reason for obsoletion is that this term represent an assay and not a GO process.
 - [ ]  The reason for obsoletion is that this represents a phenotype.
 - [ ] The reason for obsoletion is that the data from the paper for which the term was requested can be accurately described using [appropriate GO term].
 - [ ] The reason for obsoletion is that this term represents a molecular function.
 - [ ]  The reason for obsoletion is that this term represents a gene product.
 - [ ] The reason for obsoletion is that this term refers to a class of proteins.
 - [ ]  The reason for obsoletion is that this term was added in error.
 - [x]  The reason for obsoletion is that more specific terms were created.
 - [ ]  The reason for obsoletion is that this term was an unnecessary grouping term.
 - [x]  The reason for obsoletion is that the meaning of the term is ambiguous.
 - []  The reason for obsoletion is that is not known to be catalyzed by any gene product.
 - [ ]  The reason for obsoletion is that it more specific than the specificity of any known gene product.
 - []  The reason for obsoletion is that there is no evidence that this function/process/component exists.

There are 5 EXP annotations to these terms
UniProtKB:Q8LAI1 ; NIP7-1 PMID:21710975
UniProtKB:Q9SAI4 ; NIP6-1 PMID:18952773
UniProtKB: Q9SV84 ; NIP5-1 PMID:16679457
There is a ticket for nodulin 26-like intrinsic proteins (NIPs) https://github.com/geneontology/go-annotation/issues/5156 for when borate channel activity becomes available (https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/27487)

(newly created GO:0180044 borate channel activity)

UniProtKB:Q8NBS3 ; SLC4A11 PMID:15525507 (https://www.rhea-db.org/rhea/66817)
UniProtKB:Q8VYR7 ; BOR1 PMID:12447444
should be reannotated to 
GO:0080139 borate efflux transmembrane transporter activity


* **Any other information**

For borate efflux the 'most likely form of borate is the charged borate [B(OH4)]−form' tetrahydroxoborate CHEBI:41132 (PMID:34112901)
maps to https://www.rhea-db.org/rhea/66817 for mouse and human SLC4A11.

For the NIPs channels it is 
CHEBI:33118 boric acid


----
Checklist for ontology editor

***Check term usage and metadata in Protégé***
- [ ] check term usage in the ontology 
- [ ] check internal mappings: RHEA, EC, MetaCyc
- [ ] check subset usage
- [ ] check taxon constraints 

***Check annotations***
- [ ] create Annotation Review spreadsheet 
- * Annotation Review template: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1izV3q1jnq_Ybg5MJ53O_b5BCTkGtjl_Fi-cOg3-7eBE/edit#gid=0
- * Instructions: https://wiki.geneontology.org/Annotation_Review_Request
- [ ] create annotation review ticket: https://github.com/geneontology/go-annotation/issues/new?assignees=&labels=annotation+review&template=annotation-review-for-obsoletion.md&title=Review+annotations+to+%5BINSERT+GO+TERM%5D
- [ ] find external mappings (via IEAS), include in obsoletion notice  
- [ ] tag curators in Annotation Review ticket. Responsible annotator for each group: https://github.com/geneontology/go-site/blob/master/metadata/group-contacts.csv

***Notification***
- [ ] create [obsoletion announcement](https://github.com/geneontology/go-announcements/issues/new?assignees=&labels=obsoletion&template=obsoletion-notice.md&title=Obsoletion+notice%3A+%5BGO+ID%3A+term+label%5D)
- [ ] announce to GO friends (go-friends@mailman.stanford.edu)
- [ ] paste the text in the ontology ticket

name: active borate transmembrane transporter activity
namespace: molecular_function
alt_id: GO:0080138

synonym: "borate transmembrane transporter activity" BROAD []

intersection_of: GO:0022804 ! active transmembrane transporter activity

relationship: part_of GO:0035445 {source="GO_REF:0000090"} ! borate transmembrane transport
created_by: dhl

input.prompt:
I need to obsolete GO:0046715 "active borate transmembrane transporter activity" per issue #27488.

Current term stanza:
```
[Term]
id: GO:0046715
name: active borate transmembrane transporter activity
namespace: molecular_function
alt_id: GO:0080138
def: "Enables the transport of borate across a membrane against the concentration gradient." [PMID:12447444]
synonym: "borate transmembrane transporter activity" BROAD []
synonym: "borate uptake transmembrane transporter activity" RELATED []
synonym: "boron transmembrane transporter activity" RELATED []
synonym: "boron uptake transmembrane transporter activity" RELATED []
synonym: "efflux-type borate transporter" NARROW []
synonym: "efflux-type boron transporter" RELATED []
intersection_of: GO:0022804 ! active transmembrane transporter activity
intersection_of: has_primary_input CHEBI:33118 ! boric acid
relationship: part_of GO:0035445 {source="GO_REF:0000090"} ! borate transmembrane transport
created_by: dhl
creation_date: 2009-05-19T04:09:56Z
```

Context from issue:
- Obsoletion reason: "The reason for obsoletion is that the term is not clearly defined and usage has been inconsistent."
- Also: more specific terms were created, and the meaning is ambiguous
- There are two replacement terms mentioned:
  - GO:0080139 "borate efflux transmembrane transporter activity" (for BOR/SLC carriers)
  - GO:0180044 "borate channel activity" (for NIPs)
- The term has NO children and NO other terms reference it in the ontology (only 1 occurrence - the term itself)
- The alt_id GO:0080138 is within the stanza

Important: 
- Since there are TWO possible replacement terms (not one), use `consider` tags rather than `replaced_by`
- The obsoletion comment should be: "The reason for obsoletion is that the term is not clearly defined and usage has been inconsistent."

Please:
1. Checkout the term using `obo-checkout.pl src/ontology/go-edit.obo GO:0046715`
2. Edit the term file in terms/ to make it obsolete following GO conventions:
   - Prepend "obsolete " to the name
   - Add "is_obsolete: true"
   - Add comment with the obsoletion reason
   - Add consider tags for GO:0080139 and GO:0180044
   - Remove all logical axioms (intersection_of, relationship, is_a)
   - Remove synonyms
   - Keep: id, name (with "obsolete " prefix), namespace, def, alt_id, created_by, creation_date
   - Add term_tracker_item for issue 27488
3. Check it back in with `obo-checkin.pl src/ontology/go-edit.obo GO:0046715`
4. DO NOT run validation yet, I will do that separately

Do the edits now, do not just plan them.

Obsolete GO:0046715 active borate transmembrane transporter activity

The term is not clearly defined and usage has been inconsistent.
It was used to annotate both NIP channels and BOR/SLC carriers,
which are functionally distinct transport mechanisms. More specific
replacement terms exist: GO:0080139 (borate efflux transmembrane
transporter activity) and GO:0180044 (borate channel activity).

Addresses: https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/27488

---

)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Obsolete GO:0046715 active borate transmembrane transporter activity" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Obsoletes GO:0046715 "active borate transmembrane transporter activity" as requested in #27488.

The term conflated two distinct transport mechanisms — NIP channels and BOR/SLC carriers — leading to inconsistent annotation. Two more specific replacement terms are suggested via `consider` tags:

- **GO:0080139** borate efflux transmembrane transporter activity (for BOR/SLC carriers)
- **GO:0180044** borate channel activity (for NIPs)

## Changes to GO:0046715

- Prefixed name with "obsolete "
- Prefixed definition with "OBSOLETE. "
- Removed all logical axioms (`intersection_of`, `relationship`)
- Removed all synonyms
- Added `is_obsolete: true`
- Added obsoletion `comment`
- Added `consider: GO:0080139` and `consider: GO:0180044`
- Added `term_tracker_item` for #27488
- Retained `alt_id: GO:0080138`, namespace, original provenance

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN: Issue analyzed, intent clear
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION: Ontology valid before changes
- [x] TERM-SEARCH: Confirmed no other terms reference GO:0046715
- [x] EDITS: Checkout/checkin procedure followed
- [x] METADATA: term_tracker_item, consider tags, comment all present
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: `make travis_build` passes (0 violations)
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED

## Note

The issue mentions 5 EXP annotations that need reannotation — this is tracked in the annotation review process referenced in the issue body.

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: `claude-opus-4-6`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @raymond91125
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/23774358373)
